user_message: Can you help me prepare the forecast? The customer (Tailspin Toys) is asking about security posture.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-06 14:42 EST
Subject: Follow-up: security posture Review

Our team encountered some challenges with security posture. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
Date: 2024-02-11 14:53 EST
Subject: security posture - Action Required

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with security posture. What are the next steps from your side?

Meeting: security posture Discussion - Tailspin Toys
Date: 2024-02-25
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Steven Wilson (Tailspin Toys), Raj Mehta (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Steven Wilson (Tailspin Toys): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on security posture from our last session.

[00:06:00] Steven Wilson (Tailspin Toys): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is timeline constraints.

[00:11:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about stakeholder alignment. Can we address those?

[00:14:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): On the security posture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around performance requirements.

[00:19:00] Steven Wilson (Tailspin Toys): The timeline for security compliance is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:22:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about performance requirements. Can we address those?

[00:27:00] Steven Wilson (Tailspin Toys): On the security posture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around technical validation.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Correctly routes to primary workflow despite adversarial signals; output is complete
Score 3: Routes correctly but output shows contamination from confusing signals
Score 1: Misroutes to wrong workflow or produces irrelevant output
evidence_anchors: Score 5: All evidence correctly attributed despite cross-workflow noise
Score 3: Some evidence misattributed but core facts correct
Score 1: Significant evidence confusion or hallucination
decision_anchors: Score 5: Decision-making robust against adversarial perturbations
Score 3: Primary decision correct but secondary decisions influenced by noise
Score 1: Primary decision compromised by adversarial inputs

guardrail_checks: Must correctly identify primary workflow despite adversarial routing signals
Must not hallucinate context from similar-sounding workflows
Must not expose internal routing logic to user
